title: Quorum — Distributed Systems Lab
description: A deterministic distributed-systems simulator: Raft, Multi-Paxos and leaderless EPaxos consensus (egalitarian, dependency-graph ordering with fast/slow paths, SCC execution and explicit-prepare recovery), PBFT and HotStuff Byzantine fault tolerance (survive malicious replicas), Snow/Avalanche metastable consensus (Slush/Snowflake/Snowball — agreement by random subsampling instead of quorums, with a live opinion-over-time chart and Byzantine resilience), a Dynamo-style tunable-quorum store (sloppy quorums, hinted handoff, read repair, vector-clock siblings), a Chord DHT, CRDTs, gossip/SWIM failure detection, vector clocks and 2PC/3PC, an ABD linearizable register (atomic read/write storage with no consensus), Chandy–Lamport consistent global snapshots (FIFO-channel marker algorithm recording a consistent cut of a running, conserved token economy), Lamport logical-clock mutual exclusion (REQUEST/REPLY/RELEASE ordered by (timestamp,id) — distributed locking with no lock server), Bracha Byzantine reliable broadcast (SEND/ECHO/READY amplification surviving an equivocating sender at N≥3f+1), and a from-scratch Wing & Gong linearizability checker (the NP-complete decision procedure, made tractable by real-time pruning, memoized dead ends and Herlihy–Wing locality) that works for any sequential object — register/CAS/counter/set/FIFO-queue/stack/lock — certifying a passing history with a concrete witness order and convicting a failing one by naming the operation that went back in time, run on textbook counterexamples, random schedules and real ABD runs harvested live off the kernel; all from scratch on one seeded discrete-event kernel with a controllable network, time-travel scrubbing and live invariant checking.
